description: Ten curated bug-fix tasks to measure agent correctness (pass rate) rather than only structural behavior.

tasks.request: The function slice_tokens in py/src/lg_orch/memory.py uses integer division to estimate token count. When the input text is exactly 4 characters long the result is 1, but when it is 3 characters it returns 0 even though there is content. Fix approx_token_count so that any non-empty string returns at least 1.
acceptance_criteria: approx_token_count('abc') returns 1
approx_token_count('') returns 0
approx_token_count('abcd') returns 1

args: import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'py/src'); from lg_orch.memory import approx_token_count; assert approx_token_count('abc')==1 and approx_token_count('')==0, 'FAIL'

tasks.request: In py/src/lg_orch/nodes/reporter.py the _get_inference_config function calls str(oc_cfg.get('api_key', '')).strip() which converts None to 'None' when the key exists but has a null value. Fix this to treat None the same as a missing key (return empty string).
acceptance_criteria: api_key of None is treated as empty string
api_key of '' is treated as empty string
api_key of 'sk-abc' is preserved as 'sk-abc'

tasks.request: configs/runtime.dev.toml is missing the [vericoding] section. Add it with enabled = false and extensions = [".rs"] so that load_config does not fall back to a hardcoded default and all profiles are consistent.
acceptance_criteria: runtime.dev.toml contains [vericoding]
vericoding.enabled is false in dev config
load_config succeeds for dev profile

args: import sys,os; sys.path.insert(0,'py/src'); os.environ['LG_PROFILE']='dev'; from pathlib import Path; from lg_orch.config import load_config; cfg=load_config(repo_root=Path('.')); assert cfg.vericoding is not None, 'FAIL'

tasks.request: There is no test for the LG_RUNNER_BASE_URL environment variable override added to py/src/lg_orch/config.py. Add a test to py/tests/test_config.py that sets LG_RUNNER_BASE_URL to 'http://external:9000' and asserts that load_config returns that URL as runner.base_url.
acceptance_criteria: test_config.py contains a test for LG_RUNNER_BASE_URL
The test passes when the env var is set
The test confirms runner.base_url matches the env override

tasks.request: The stable_checkpoint_thread_id function in py/src/lg_orch/checkpointing.py strips whitespace from the provided thread_id but does not validate it further. If the provided value is only whitespace it falls through to the generated ID. Add a test that confirms a whitespace-only provided value is treated as missing and the function generates a deterministic ID instead.
acceptance_criteria: stable_checkpoint_thread_id with provided='   ' generates a deterministic ID
Generated ID starts with the thread_prefix

tasks.request: In py/src/lg_orch/policy.py the enforce_loop_budget function reads current_loop from budgets dict. If budgets contains a float (e.g. 1.5) the coerce function rounds it down. Add a test confirming that a float value of 1.5 is treated as 1 and does not cause a TypeError.
acceptance_criteria: enforce_loop_budget with budgets={'max_loops':3,'current_loop':1.5} does not raise
The result treats current_loop as 1

tasks.request: The route_after_verifier function in py/src/lg_orch/graph.py reads state.get('verification', {}).get('ok') but VerifierReport serialises to a dict with the key 'ok'. Add a test that passes a VerifierReport with ok=True and confirms the router returns 'reporter', and ok=False returns 'policy_gate'.
acceptance_criteria: route_after_verifier({'verification': {'ok': True}}) == 'reporter'
route_after_verifier({'verification': {'ok': False}}) == 'policy_gate'
route_after_verifier({}) == 'policy_gate'

tasks.request: The __all__ list in py/src/lg_orch/memory.py includes 'ensure_history_policy' but a ruff --select F401 pass would flag that the symbol is used internally and not really needed in __all__. Verify the export list is correct and that importing all symbols via 'from lg_orch.memory import *' works without NameError.
acceptance_criteria: from lg_orch.memory import * does not raise NameError
All symbols in __all__ are defined in the module

args: import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'py/src'); from lg_orch.memory import *; print('ok')

tasks.request: The _CircuitBreaker in py/src/lg_orch/tools/inference_client.py uses a threading.Lock. Add a test that runs 50 concurrent threads each calling allow_request() and record_failure() and asserts no exceptions are raised (thread-safety smoke test).
acceptance_criteria: 50 concurrent threads can call allow_request and record_failure without errors
The circuit breaker opens after >= 5 failures

tasks.request: The streaming_sync wrapper chat_completion_stream_sync in py/src/lg_orch/tools/inference_client.py swallows StreamError and falls back to chat_completion. Add a unit test that mocks chat_completion_stream to raise RuntimeError and asserts that chat_completion_stream_sync calls the blocking fallback instead of propagating the error.
acceptance_criteria: chat_completion_stream_sync falls back to chat_completion on stream error
The fallback result text is returned correctly
